commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ClawHub exact-release trust checks, acknowledgement UX, persisted trust metadata, Gateway/UI error details, docs, and tests for plugin and skill install/update paths.

PR surface: Source +2530, Tests +4336, Docs +38, Other +21. Total +6925 across 70 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: PR-head source shows every non-ClawHub plugin install logs the new future-blocking warning. For the central hardening, current-main source shows the missing pre-download trust gate rather than a failing runtime repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is required for the protected maintainer label, dirty merge state, fail-closed trust rollout, persisted metadata, and the remaining scoped warning finding.

target.title: Check ClawHub trust before plugin and skill installs
target.body:
## Summary OpenClaw now checks ClawHub exact-release trust before downloading ClawHub plugin releases and archive-backed ClawHub skill releases. Malicious or ClawHub-blocked releases fail closed before download, suspicious/review-required releases require acknowledgement, and pending or stale non-risk trust states warn without blocking. > [!IMPORTANT] > Official/source-linked ClawHub metadata is a trust signal, not a security bypass. `@openclaw/kitchen-sink` can be official/source-linked and still follows the exact release trust verdict before install or update proceeds. ## What Changed | Surface | New behavior | | --- | --- | | Plugin installs | Check ClawHub release trust before archive download; blocked/malicious releases do not download. | | Skill installs | Apply the same release trust gate to archive-backed ClawHub skills, including owner-qualified `@owner/slug` refs. | | Plugin and skill updates | Leave the installed item unchanged when the latest release is malicious, blocked, risky without acknowledgement, or security-unavailable. | | Acknowledgement UX | Suspicious/review-required releases require either an interactive acknowledgement or `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk`. | | Official metadata | Persist and display source-linked/official metadata, but do not let it bypass release security. | | Fallbacks | Preserve availability-only artifact fallback, but do not fall back for trust, integrity, malicious, or blocked failures. | | Docs | Update ClawHub, plugin, skill, and update CLI docs for the new trust gate and acknowledgement flag. | ## Proof Behavioral proof is tied to PR head `b08ced424daa970c466e2fd80aca944ba5f4ac54`. Plugin install: - Malicious or ClawHub-blocked release: `openclaw plugins install clawhub:` exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, and does not install. - Suspicious release: without acknowledgement exits `1`; with `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` exits `0` and keeps the warning visible. - Clean release: community and official/source-linked plugin releases install successfully. Official metadata does not bypass the exact release trust check. Skill install: - Malicious or ClawHub-blocked release: `openclaw skills install @owner/ --global` exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, and does not install. - Suspicious release: without acknowledgement exits `1`; with `--acknowledge-clawhub-risk` exits `0` and keeps the warning visible. - Clean release: community and official/source-linked skill releases install successfully. Owner-qualified skills use `@owner/slug` in warnings and acknowledgements. Update and repair paths: - Malicious latest plugin or skill version: the update does not download the latest release and tells the user to uninstall the installed item unless independently reviewed. - Security unavailable or trust failure: installs fail closed; update, doctor repair, onboarding repair, and post-core sync leave the installed item unchanged and report the trust/security failure. - Dry-run updates: do not create an interactive ClawHub risk prompt. Chat and Control UI: - Chat `/plugins install ...`: risky ClawHub plugin installs cannot be acknowledged from chat and tell the user to use a trusted local shell command. - Chat skills: there is no `/skills install` chat command path in this PR; chat `/skill [input]` only invokes existing skills. - Control UI skill install: malicious/suspicious skill releases fail before download; suspicious releases surface as structured errors with acknowledgement retry support.
End-to-end install transcript matrix | Scenario | Command surface | Result | | --- | --- | --- | | malicious / blocked plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:` | exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, does not install | | suspicious plugin release without acknowledgement | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:` | exits `1`, prints `WARNING`, does not install | | suspicious plugin release with acknowledgement | `openclaw plugins install clawhub: --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` | exits `0`, warning remains visible | | clean community plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:` | exits `0` | | clean official/source-linked plugin release | `openclaw plugins install clawhub:` | exits `0`; official metadata does not bypass release trust | | malicious / blocked skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/ --global` | exits `1`, prints `BLOCKED`, does not install | | suspicious skill release without acknowledgement | `openclaw skills install @owner/ --global` | exits `1`, prints `WARNING`, does not install | | suspicious skill release with acknowledgement | `openclaw skills install @owner/ --global --acknowledge-clawhub-risk` | exits `0`, warning remains visible | | clean community skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/ --global` | exits `0` | | clean official/source-linked skill release | `openclaw skills install @owner/ --global` | exits `0`; official metadata does not bypass release trust |
Warning examples Suspicious plugin warning: ```text WARNING - ClawHub found security risks in this release - Security scan: suspicious - Finding: suspicious payload strings This plugin is not marked malicious, but ClawHub found security findings or a large local system blast radius. Review the ClawHub security details before installing. ``` Suspicious skill warning: ```text WARNING - ClawHub found security risks in this release - Security scan: suspicious - Finding: security status is not clean This skill is not marked malicious, but ClawHub found security findings or a large instruction/tool-use blast radius. Review the ClawHub security details before installing. ``` Blocked release warning: ```text BLOCKED - ClawHub flagged this release as malicious - Security scan: malicious - Moderation: quarantined - Finding: manual_moderation - Scanner: malicious behavior detected - Finding: Download disabled by ClawHub for this release ``` Malicious latest update warning: ```text Latest plugin version is marked malicious; OpenClaw will not download it. Uninstall the installed plugin unless you have independently reviewed it. Latest skill version is marked malicious; OpenClaw will not download it. Uninstall the installed skill unless you have independently reviewed it. ``` Raw static-analysis and VirusTotal links are intentionally not printed.
Additional edge cases covered - Review-required or failed verdict: plugin install/update and skill install/update require typing the target package or skill label before continuing. - Review-recommended release: plugin install/update and skill install/update use yes/no confirmation before continuing. - Pending or stale non-risk trust: plugin install/update warnings and JSON update output warn without requiring acknowledgement. - ClawHub security unavailable: plugin install, skill install, plugin update, skill update, doctor, onboarding, and update repair leave the user with a clear failure instead of a silent install. - Owner-qualified skills: skill install/update, warnings, retry payloads, and links validate publisher identity and use `@owner/slug`. - Trust failure fallback: plugin install/update and doctor repair do not fall back to npm/default fallback for malicious, blocked, integrity, or trust failures. - Availability fallback: official ClawHub artifact misses can still fall back to trusted npm packages where the existing availability-only fallback already allowed it. - Dry-run updates: plugin update and `openclaw update` post-core work avoid interactive ClawHub risk prompts. - Onboarding and doctor repair: acknowledgement guidance is forwarded and risky ClawHub releases are not silently installed.
## Screenshots > [!NOTE] > PR-visible screenshots are not embedded because the Computer Use upload path is blocked by browser GitHub login state: fresh agent-owned Chrome windows for both local Chrome profiles reached this PR but only showed signed-out GitHub controls and `Sign in to comment`, so GitHub's PR comment attachment UI was unavailable. The refreshed command transcript excerpts above and focused tests cover the terminal, update, repair, chat, Gateway, and Control UI behavior. ## Verification Reviewer-checkable behavior: - `node .artifacts/clawhub-install-trust/run-proof.cjs` passed and regenerated the 10-scenario plugin/skill trust command transcript at PR head `b08ced424daa970c466e2fd80aca944ba5f4ac54`. - The proof-pack net-diff script passed and confirmed there is no branch-only churn with no net diff. - Live GitHub currently reports the PR open but merge-conflicting against `main`; it needs a branch refresh before final landing. Automated and static checks: - `git diff --check 106961b51351e3f6cbb10839cb357b130e1a477b...HEAD` passed. - `pnpm docs:list` passed. - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/clawhub.test.ts src/plugins/update.test.ts src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/skills.clawhub.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/skills.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/skills.test.ts` passed: 360 tests. - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/clawhub-risk-acknowledgement.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.install.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.commands.test.ts src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/cli/update-cli/post-core-plugin-convergence.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/missing-configured-plugin-install.test.ts src/commands/onboarding-plugin-install.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-plugins.install.test.ts` passed: 546 tests. Not run locally: full `pnpm check`, full build, Crabbox/Testbox, live ClawHub install scenarios, or browser screenshot audit. CI should cover broader gates after the branch is refreshed.
